attributes.system_prompt:
You are Natively, an AI assistant that helps users build mobile apps. If asked who you are, say you're Natively and that you help build mobile apps — nothing more.

## Principles

attributes.gen_ai.skill.name: better-auth
attributes.gen_ai.skill.content:
# Better Auth + Specular Authentication

Complete guide for setting up authentication in an Expo app using Better Auth on the frontend and Specular as the backend.

## References

Consult these templates when creating auth files:

```
references/
  auth-client.md       lib/auth.ts — Better Auth client configuration, token storage
  api-utils.md         utils/api.ts — Authenticated API helpers (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE)
  auth-context.md      contexts/AuthContext.tsx — AuthProvider, useAuth hook, OAuth flows
  auth-screens.md      Auth popup, callback page, auth screen with email + social providers
```

## Overview

The authentication system creates these files:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `lib/auth.ts` | Better Auth client with Expo plugin, token storage |
| `utils/api.ts` | API call helpers with automatic Bearer token injection |
| `contexts/AuthContext.tsx` | React context providing `useAuth()` hook |
| `app/auth-popup.tsx` | OAuth popup that initiates social sign-in |
| `app/auth-callback.tsx` | OAuth callback that receives token and posts to parent window |
| Auth screen (you create) | Sign in / sign up screen — styled to match the app's design |

## Prerequisites

### 1. Install Dependencies

These packages must be installed:

```
better-auth @better-auth/expo expo-secure-store expo-linking expo-constants expo-web-browser expo-apple-authentication
```

**iOS Apple Sign-In capability**: Installing `expo-apple-authentication` alone is not enough. The native Apple Sign-In entitlement must be enabled. Add the following plugin to `app.json`:

```json
{
  "expo": {
    "plugins": ["expo-apple-authentication"]
  }
}
```

This ensures the `com.apple.developer.applesignin` entitlement is included in the iOS build.

### 2. App Scheme

`app.json` must have a `scheme` property under `expo`:

```json
{
  "expo": {
    "scheme": "myapp"
  }
}
```

Read the scheme from `app.json` (`expo.scheme`) and use it in the auth client and context.

### 3. Backend Configuration (Critical)

Do NOT set `trustedOrigins`, `baseURL`, or any other advanced Better Auth config in the `make_backend_change` prompt — let Specular use its standard defaults.

Only request: email/password, Google provider, Apple provider, and the required database tables.

## OAuth Flow — How It Works

### Native (iOS/Android)

1. `authClient.signIn.social({ provider, callbackURL: "scheme://auth-callback" })` is called with a fully-qualified deep link URI
2. expoClient opens an in-app browser via `expo-web-browser` (`ASWebAuthenticationSession` on iOS, Custom Tabs on Android)
3. Better Auth redirects to the OAuth provider (Google, Apple, etc.)
4. User authenticates with the provider
5. Provider redirects back to Better Auth → session is established
6. Better Auth redirects to the deep link callback URL → browser closes
7. `signIn.social()` promise resolves → app calls `fetchUser()` to load the session

### Web (popup-based)

1. App opens a popup window to `/auth-popup?provider=google`
2. Popup calls `authClient.signIn.social({ provider, callbackURL: "/auth-callback" })`
3. User authenticates with the provider in the popup
4. Provider redirects back to Better Auth → session cookie set
5. Better Auth redirects to `/auth-callback?better_auth_token=<token>`
6. Callback page reads the token and sends it to the parent window via `window.postMessage`
7. Parent window receives the token, saves it, and calls `fetchUser()`

## Configuration

| Parameter | Default | Description |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| `backend_url` | `Constants.expoConfig?.extra?.backendUrl` | Backend API URL |
| `app_name` | `"expo-project"` | Storage key prefix. Must only contain alphanumeric chars, `.`, `-`, `_` (SecureStore requirement). Use the `expo.scheme` value from `app.json`. |
| `scheme` | from `app.json` `expo.scheme` | Deep link scheme |
| `providers` | `["google", "apple"]` | OAuth providers to enable |

## Integration

### Wrap Root Layout with AuthProvider

```tsx
import { AuthProvider } from "@/contexts/AuthContext";

export default function RootLayout() {
  return (
    <AuthProvider>
      {/* rest of the app */}
    </AuthProvider>
  );
}
```

### Use the useAuth Hook

```tsx
import { useAuth } from "@/contexts/AuthContext";

function ProfileScreen() {
  const { user, loading, signOut } = useAuth();

  if (loading) return <ActivityIndicator />;
  if (!user) return <Redirect href="/auth-screen" />;

  return (
    <View>
      <Text>{user.name}</Text>
      <Button title="Sign Out" onPress={signOut} />
    </View>
  );
}
```

### Available Auth Methods

The `useAuth()` hook exposes:

- `user` — current user object or null
- `loading` — whether auth state is being determined
- `signInWithEmail(email, password)` — email/password sign in
- `signUpWithEmail(email, password, name?)` — create account
- `signInWithApple()` — Apple Sign In (native modal on iOS, OAuth on web/Android) — **must appear first**
- `signInWithGoogle()` — Google OAuth
- `signOut()` — sign out and clear tokens
- `fetchUser()` — refresh the current user

## Backend Setup via Specular

Specular already has OAuth credentials pre-configured for Google and Apple.
Do NOT ask the user to set up `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, `APPLE_CLIENT_ID`, `APPLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET`, `BETTER_AUTH_URL`, or any other OAuth environment variables. These are handled automatically by Specular.

When setting up auth, call `make_backend_change` with a simple prompt — keep it close to this format:

```
Set up Better Auth authentication with:
- Email/password authentication (signup, login, password reset)
- Google OAuth provider
- Apple OAuth provider
Database: Create user, session, account, and verification tables as needed by Better Auth
```

Do NOT include `trustedOrigins`, `baseURL`, or any other advanced Better Auth config — Specular handles these automatically.

## File Creation Order

1. Read `app.json` to get the `expo.scheme` value
2. Call `setup_auth` tool — generates `lib/auth.ts`, `utils/api.ts`, `contexts/AuthContext.tsx`, `app/auth-popup.tsx`, `app/auth-callback.tsx`
3. Create the auth screen yourself (e.g. `app/auth-screen.tsx`) — styled to match the app's design, using `useAuth()` from `@/contexts/AuthContext`
4. Update root `_layout.tsx` to wrap with `<AuthProvider>`
5. Install dependencies

attributes.gen_ai.agent.name: natively
attributes.gen_ai.agent.spawn.task:
Diagnose and fix a Google OAuth authentication issue on Android APK. When the user tries to sign in with Google, they see "procesando autenticación" and then "no se encontró sesión, por favor intenta de nuevo".

This is a classic Android deep link / OAuth callback issue. Please:

1. Read `app.json` to get the scheme
2. Read `lib/auth.ts` (or `lib/auth.tsx`) to see the auth client configuration
3. Read `contexts/AuthContext.tsx` to see how `signInWithGoogle` is implemented
4. Read `app/auth-callback.tsx` (or `app/auth/callback.tsx`) to see the callback handler
5. Read `app/login.tsx` or the auth screen to see how the Google button calls the auth

Then fix the following common Android OAuth issues:

**Issue 1 - callbackURL must be a full deep link URI on native:**
In `signInWithGoogle` (and `signInWithApple`), the `callbackURL` passed to `authClient.signIn.social()` must be the full deep link URI like `scheme://auth-callback`, NOT a relative path like `/auth-callback`. On Android, a relative path causes the session to not be found.

**Issue 2 - The callback screen must call `fetchUser()` after receiving the token:**
In `app/auth-callback.tsx`, after the OAuth redirect lands, the screen must:
- On native: call `authClient.getSession()` or `fetchUser()` from AuthContext to refresh the session
- Make sure it's not just showing a loading spinner and doing nothing

**Issue 3 - expo-web-browser must be properly dismissed:**
After `signIn.social()` resolves on Android, `WebBrowser.maybeCompleteAuthSession()` must be called at the top of the callback screen.

**Issue 4 - The auth client `fetchHeaders` must include the token:**
Verify `lib/auth.ts` has proper token storage and retrieval so the session can be fetched after OAuth completes.

Apply all necessary fixes. The app scheme from `app.json` must be used as the deep link scheme (e.g. if scheme is "nospi", callbackURL should be "nospi://auth-callback").

After fixing, make sure the Google sign-in flow on Android works end-to-end:
1. User taps Google sign-in
2. Browser opens Google OAuth
3. User authenticates
4. App receives deep link callback
5. Session is fetched and user is logged in
6. User is redirected to the main app

attributes.content:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me analyze the root causes:

**Root cause analysis:**

1. **`app.json` intentFilter has `host: "auth"` but the callback route is `auth/callback`** — the deep link `nospi://auth/callback` won't match `nospi://auth` as a host. The host should be `auth-callback` or the scheme needs to match the actual route path.

2. **`Linking.createURL('auth/callback')` on Android produces `nospi://auth/callback`** — but the intentFilter only registers `nospi://auth` as a valid deep link host, so Android won't route it back to the app.

3. **`flowType: 'pkce'` + `detectSessionInUrl: false` on native** — with PKCE, Supabase sends a `code` param (not tokens) in the redirect. The callback screen only looks for `access_token`/`refresh_token` and never handles the `code` exchange, so it always falls through to the 8-second timeout and fails.

4. **The callback screen never calls `supabase.auth.exchangeCodeForSession()`** — this is the critical missing piece for PKCE on native.

5. **`WebBrowser.openAuthSessionAsync` on Android** — when the browser redirects back, the result URL contains the `code` param. The login screen tries to extract tokens from it (which don't exist in PKCE), then navigates to `/auth/callback` without passing the `code`.

Let me fix all of this:

attributes.gen_ai.agent.name: frontend
attributes.content:
Now I have a complete picture. Here's the full diagnosis:

**Root causes:**

1. **PKCE `code` never exchanged** — `flowType: 'pkce'` in `lib/supabase.ts` means Supabase redirects back with `?code=...`, NOT `access_token`/`refresh_token`. The callback screen only looks for tokens and never calls `exchangeCodeForSession(code)`, so it always falls through to the 8-second timeout → "no se encontró sesión".

2. **`app.json` intentFilter has `host: "auth"` but the deep link is `nospi://auth/callback`** — Android won't intercept the redirect because the host `auth` doesn't match the path segment `auth` in `nospi://auth/callback` (which has host=`auth` and path=`/callback`). Actually the host IS `auth` here, but the intentFilter is missing the `pathPrefix: "/callback"` data entry, so it may not match precisely.

3. **Login/Register screens try to extract tokens from the `WebBrowser` result URL** — with PKCE the URL has `?code=...`, not tokens. They pass nothing useful to the callback screen.

4. **`detectSessionInUrl: false` on native** — Supabase won't auto-process the code from the URL, so it must be done manually.

**Fix strategy:** Extract the `code` from the callback URL in the login/register screens and pass it to the callback screen, which then calls `exchangeCodeForSession(code)`. Also fix the intentFilter in `app.json` to properly match `nospi://auth/callback`.

attributes.gen_ai.agent.name: frontend
attributes.content:
Only a lint warning (not an error) on the empty `useEffect` deps array — that's intentional since we only want it to run once on mount. All fixes are complete.

Here's a summary of every root cause found and fixed:

---

**Root cause 1 — PKCE `code` never exchanged (the main bug)**
`lib/supabase.ts` uses `flowType: 'pkce'`, so Supabase redirects back with `?code=...`, not `access_token`/`refresh_token`. The old callback screen only looked for tokens and never called `exchangeCodeForSession()`, so it always fell through to the 8-second timeout → "no se encontró sesión".

**Fix:** `app/auth/callback.tsx` now calls `supabase.auth.exchangeCodeForSession(code)` as the primary path (Step 4), with token-based and `getSession()` fallbacks below it.

**Root cause 2 — Login/Register screens discarded the `code`**
`login.tsx` and `register.tsx` tried to extract `access_token`/`refresh_token` from the `WebBrowser` result URL (which don't exist in PKCE), then navigated to `/auth/callback` with nothing useful.

**Fix:** Both screens now extract `code` from the callback URL first and pass it as a route param to `/auth/callback`.

**Root cause 3 — `app.json` intentFilter missing `pathPrefix`**
The Android intentFilter registered `nospi://auth` (no path), but the deep link is `nospi://auth/callback`. Android wouldn't intercept the redirect precisely.

**Fix:** Added `"pathPrefix": "/callback"` to the `nospi` scheme entry in `intentFilters`.

**Root cause 4 — `WebBrowser.maybeCompleteAuthSession()` missing from callback screen**
Required on Android to signal the custom tab to close after the redirect lands.

**Fix:** Added `WebBrowser.maybeCompleteAuthSession()` at the top of `app/auth/callback.tsx`.

---

**End-to-end flow now:**
1. User taps Google → `WebBrowser.openAuthSessionAsync` opens Chrome Custom Tab
2. Google authenticates → redirects to `nospi://auth/callback?code=...`
3. Android intentFilter intercepts → app opens `/auth/callback` with `code` param
4. `exchangeCodeForSession(code)` exchanges it for a real session
5. Profile is checked/created → user navigated to `/(tabs)/events`

> **Note:** A new APK build is required for the `intentFilter` change in `app.json` to take effect on Android.

attributes.system_prompt:
You are Natively, an AI assistant that helps users build mobile apps. If asked who you are, say you're Natively and that you help build mobile apps — nothing more.

## Principles
- You are an orchestrator — you have NO code editing tools. ALL implementation MUST go through spawn_agent.
- Do NOT respond with text suggestions or instructions — use spawn_agent.
- Understand the request against the project summary before acting.
- Only enter plan mode (plan_enter) when the user explicitly asks to pl

attributes.gen_ai.agent.name: natively
attributes.content:
Los fixes están aplicados. Los problemas principales eran:

1. El código PKCE de Google nunca se intercambiaba por una sesión
2. Las pantallas de login descartaban el `code` del callback
3. El `intentFilter` de Android no capturaba la ruta `/auth/callback` correctamente

Necesitas generar un nuevo APK para que el cambio en `app.json` (intentFilter) tome efecto.
